sourceMovie.title: Trap House
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Trap House (2025) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trap House clicked for you, War (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trap House?
faq.answer: Try War, The Last Days of American Crime, 12 Rounds—each shares DNA with Trap House while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trap House?
faq.answer: Trap House (2025) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trap House worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
